summary: Russian Shahed‑type UAVs launched large‑scale attacks across Ukraine, with 110 drones shot down and multiple strikes on Sumy, Dnipro and Zaporizhzhia facilities. Russia also struck the Temryuk sea port in Krasnodar Krai, causing one death and several injuries. Ukrainian defenses continue to report high UAV activity along northern borders and threats of medium‑range ballistic missiles from Kapustin Yar.
key_points: Russian Shahed and other UAVs attacked Sumy farm, killing five workers and damaging livestock, and also hit a DTEK power plant killing one and wounding another (Source: @UkraineNow/81883).
A Russian strike on the Temryuk sea port in Krasnodar Krai caused a fire, one fatality and three injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81889).
Ukrainian forces reported shooting down 110 hostile UAVs of various types on the night of 12‑13 June (Source: @kpszsu/64976).
Multiple UAV groups were observed moving from northern Chernihiv and Kyiv regions toward central Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/64964, @kpszsu/64948, @kpszsu/64954).
Ukrainian authorities warned of a high probability of a ballistic missile launch from the Kapustin Yar test site (Source: @UkraineNow/81873).
Russian air defense claimed to have downed 177 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory and Black Sea/Azov Sea areas (Source: @tass_agency/380464).
Ukrainian officials announced upcoming reforms to military service terms and increased pay (Source: @UkraineNow/81872).

sections.executive_summary: Russian Shahed‑type UAVs carried out extensive attacks across Ukraine, with 110 drones intercepted and strikes on civilian and energy targets in Sumy and Zaporizhzhia. A Russian missile strike hit the strategic Temryuk sea port in Krasnodar, killing one and injuring several. Ukrainian defenses continue to monitor high UAV traffic along northern borders and anticipate possible ballistic missile launches from Kapustin Yar.

points: Ukrainian forces shot down or suppressed 110 hostile UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera and Iltamys types, across northern, southern and eastern sectors on the night of 12‑13 June (Source: @kpszsu/64976).
A Russian UAV strike on a farm in Sumy region killed five workers, injured livestock, and caused additional casualties at a DTEK power plant (one dead, one wounded) (Source: @UkraineNow/81883).
A Russian drone hit the Nova Poshta terminal in Zaporizhzhia, causing a fire but no reported casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81871).
Ukrainian air defense reported downing 177 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory and Black Sea/Azov Sea areas, reflecting high UAV activity on both sides (Source: @tass_agency/380464).
Ukrainian officials warned of a likely launch of a ballistic missile from the Kapustin Yar test site within 24 hours (Source: @UkraineNow/81873).
Russian forces struck the Temryuk sea port in Krasnodar Krai, igniting a fire, killing one civilian and wounding three others (Source: @UkraineNow/81889).
Russian air defense claimed to have shot down 177 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions and maritime zones (Source: @tass_agency/380464).
A bomb explosion occurred at the former DNR security minister Pinchuk's residence in Nova Moscow, injuring Pinchuk (Source: @tass_agency/380432).
Russian media reported a failed attempt to introduce visas for Russian citizens in Bosnia and Herzegovina, reflecting diplomatic tensions (Source: @tass_agency/380454).
Russian officials warned of potential ballistic missile launches from Kapustin Yar, indicating continued offensive missile readiness (Source: @UkraineNow/81873).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV groups moving from Zaporizhzhia toward Dnipropetrovsk and from Sumy northward, indicating pressure on the eastern axis (Source: @kpszsu/64969).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Sumy region (Source: @kpszsu/64930).
Ukrainian air defense intercepted multiple Shahed drones over the eastern front, with three impact points recorded (Source: @kpszsu/64976).
Ukrainian officials noted a high likelihood of a ballistic missile strike from Kapustin Yar affecting eastern territories (Source: @UkraineNow/81873).
Ukrainian radar reported UAV activity near the Dnipro River corridor, including groups from Krivyi Rih district heading toward Kamianets (Source: @kpszsu/64980).
UAVs were observed flying along the Belarus border in northern Chernihiv region, heading toward Kyiv oblast (Source: @kpszsu/64964, @kpszsu/64948).
Multiple UAV groups were reported near the Chernobyl exclusion zone moving westward (Source: @kpszsu/64954).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV activity from the north of Zhytomyr heading toward Korosten (Source: @kpszsu/64973).
UAVs were sighted along the Dnipro highway in the Kyiv region, indicating surveillance of northern approaches (Source: @kpszsu/64954).
Ukrainian air defense claimed to have downed several UAVs over northern sectors during the 110‑drone interception event (Source: @kpszsu/64976).
Russian strike on the Temryuk sea port in Krasnodar Krai caused casualties and infrastructure damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81889).
Ukrainian reports of UAV groups moving from Zaporizhzhia toward Dnipropetrovsk suggest pressure on the southern front (Source: @kpszsu/64969).
Ukrainian forces noted UAV activity near the Black Sea and Azov Sea areas, aligning with Russian claims of downed Ukrainian drones (Source: @tass_agency/380464).
A Russian explosion at a former DNR minister's residence in Nova Moscow, while not a front line event, reflects broader security concerns in the south (Source: @tass_agency/380432).
Ukrainian authorities warned of possible medium‑range ballistic missile launches affecting southern regions (Source: @UkraineNow/81873).
Ukrainian forces intercepted 110 hostile UAVs of various types on the night of 12‑13 June, with three UAVs hitting targets and debris falling in six locations (Source: @kpszsu/64976).
Russian air defense reported shooting down 177 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory and maritime zones (Source: @tass_agency/380464).
Ukrainian radar recorded multiple Shahed‑type UAV launches from areas including Oril, Kursk, Millerovo, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, Donetsk, and Crimea (Source: @kpszsu/64976).
Ukrainian officials warned of imminent ballistic missile launches from Kapustin Yar, indicating a potential escalation in missile use (Source: @UkraineNow/81873).

Ukrainian air defense showcased a promotional video of AI‑enabled interceptors in Kharkiv, though subsequent drone strikes raised questions about operational effectiveness (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30863).

key_developments: Russian Shahed‑type UAVs launched a coordinated attack on Sumy farm and a DTEK power plant, causing five worker deaths and one plant fatality (Source: @UkraineNow/81883).
Ukrainian forces shot down or suppressed 110 hostile UAVs across multiple fronts on 12‑13 June, including Shahed, Gerbera and Iltamys models (Source: @kpszsu/64976).
A Russian missile strike hit the strategic Temryuk sea port in Krasnodar, killing one civilian and injuring three (Source: @UkraineNow/81889).
Ukrainian radar detected multiple UAV groups moving from northern Chernihiv and Kyiv regions toward central Ukraine, indicating increased surveillance activity (Source: @kpszsu/64964, @kpszsu/64948).
Russian air defense claimed to have downed 177 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory and Black Sea/Azov Sea areas (Source: @tass_agency/380464).
Ukrainian officials warned of a high probability of a ballistic missile launch from the Kapustin Yar test site within 24 hours (Source: @UkraineNow/81873).
Ukrainian Ministry of Defense announced upcoming reforms to service terms and pay increases for military personnel (Source: @UkraineNow/81872).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian forces face a sustained UAV threat, with Russian Shahed and other drone types conducting mass attacks that have caused civilian and infrastructure casualties. The reported possibility of a medium‑range ballistic missile launch from Kapustin Yar adds a new layer of risk, potentially targeting critical rear areas. Russian strikes on strategic assets such as the Temryuk port demonstrate continued capability to project power beyond the front lines, while Ukrainian air defenses show resilience but are strained by the volume of attacks.
sections.regional_response: Northern Ukrainian regions are intensifying air surveillance as UAVs approach from the Belarus border, prompting heightened alert levels. Eastern command centers are focusing on intercepting guided bomb deliveries and mitigating UAV incursions toward key logistics hubs. Southern authorities are reinforcing coastal defenses after the Temryuk port strike, while also preparing for possible missile threats from Kapustin Yar.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV flight paths suggest coordinated launch points in Russian‑occupied territories, including Donetsk, Crimea and southern Russian regions, indicating a broad launch network. The high number of intercepted drones points to an increased production or supply line for Shahed‑type UAVs. Russian statements about missile readiness at Kapustin Yar may be a signaling effort to pressure Ukrainian defensive postures ahead of upcoming diplomatic negotiations.
